author: Etienne Dechamps
message:
Document the Windows audio buffer size as 10 ms

After some double checking it turns out 20 ms is wrong - the buffer size
that the Windows Audio Engine uses internally is 10 ms (this is the size
of the buffer that APOs are called with, and it's also the "packet" size
it uses with WaveRT hardware).

20 ms may have been correct at some point in the distant past (Windows 7
maybe?), but it's clearly not anymore.

author: Etienne Dechamps
message:
Replace unique_lock with scoped_lock

unique_lock is overkill for what we're using it for.

Fixes #235

author: Etienne Dechamps
message:
Reset outputReady as late as possible

This makes the logic a bit clearer and reduces the likelihood that we'll
misinterpret an OutputReady signal as referring to a future bufferSwitch
call.

author: Etienne Dechamps

author: Etienne Dechamps
message: Mention KoordASIO in the README

author: Etienne Dechamps
message:
Fix GetLatencies() not working on streams

This fixes a regression introduced by a typo in
59f75ce183431f8b50f7f209790090c5a608b08e.

The reason why that obviously wrong code compiled is because of a type
safety trainwreck: PaStream* is an alias of void*, which can be
implicitly converted from anything, and we accidentally passed a
PaStream** instead of a PaStream*. Oops.

Fixes #231

author: Etienne Dechamps
message:
Update ASIOTest

This brings in the test code that surfaces #231.

author: Etienne Dechamps
message:
Fix PortAudio being linked statically

Looks like at some point the default flipped and FlexASIO ended up
linking PortAudio statically instead of a separate DLL.

PortAudio/portaudio@ff75da5d80baebef837b9b498b7017c0ddece206 is a likely
culprit.

author: Etienne Dechamps
message: Use std::system_error consistently

author: Etienne Dechamps
message: Stop a debouncing config watcher

author: Etienne Dechamps
message:
Rewrite Config::Watcher

This uses simpler and more robust error handling, and fixes issues where
we were not properly completing overlapped operations when stopping the
watcher, potentially causing undefined behavior.

Fixes #209

author: Etienne Dechamps
message: Use std::byte for the config watcher buffer

author: Etienne Dechamps
message:
Check sample rates during exclusive streaming

Since we are now using Pa_IsFormatSupported() instead of Pa_OpenStream()
to probe sample rates, we should be able to actually probe sample rates
even while an exclusive stream is running. Previously, we would just
report every sample rate as supported in this scenario.

See #188. Fixes #66 (differently)

author: Etienne Dechamps
message:
Use Pa_IsFormatSupported in CanSampleRate

This should make CanSampleRate more efficient and less disruptive (e.g.
if exclusive streams are used) since the answer can be provided without
opening an actual stream.

Fixes #188
